nodes.name: Slack Slash Command
nodes.notes: Register this URL as the Request URL for the /new-opp slash command in your Slack app config. IMPORTANT: enable 'Raw Body' under Options so the Code node below can verify the Slack signature against the exact bytes Slack sent.

nodes.name: Verify Slack Signature
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER: SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET must be set as an n8n environment variable - pull this from your Slack app's Basic Information page.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "response_type": "ephemeral", "text": "Request could not be verified. Please try the command again." } }}

nodes.name: Notify Signature Failure
nodes.notes: Branch ends here - initial webhook ack was already sent, so this posts a follow-up error via response_url.

parameters.url: =https://PLACEHOLDER_SFDC_INSTANCE_URL.my.salesforce.com/services/data/v61.0/query?q={{ encodeURIComponent("SELECT Id, Name, Account.Name, StageName, Owner.Email, PLACEHOLDER_RVP_RELATIONSHIP_NAME.Email, PLACEHOLDER_REGIONAL_SE_DIRECTOR_RELATIONSHIP_NAME.Email, PLACEHOLDER_PRIMARY_SE_RELATIONSHIP_NAME.Email, PLACEHOLDER_SECONDARY_SE_RELATIONSHIP_NAME.Email FROM Opportunity WHERE Id = '" + $json.sfdcOpportunityId + "'") }}

nodes.name: Get Salesforce Opportunity
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER: replace PLACEHOLDER_SFDC_INSTANCE_URL with your Salesforce instance hostname, and the four PLACEHOLDER_*_RELATIONSHIP_NAME tokens with the real API relationship names for those lookup/role fields in your org (they're custom fields here, not standard SFDC fields).

nodes.name: Extract Opportunity Fields
nodes.notes: SOQL responses wrap results in a 'records' array, hence records[0] below. Field names map to Owner (AE), RVP (Sales Manager), Regional SE Director (SE Manager), Primary SE Assigned, Secondary SE Assigned - SDR intentionally excluded. secondarySeEmail is expected to sometimes be null/absent, handled downstream.

nodes.name: Derive Channel Variables
nodes.notes: Builds the Slack channel name/topic from the opportunity fields above (opp-<account>-<opportunity>, truncated to Slack's 80-char channel name limit). Role-email-to-Slack-ID resolution happens separately, in the Build Email List -> Look up a user by email branch below, so a single bad/missing email can't fail the whole run.

nodes.name: Create Slack Channel
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER credential: bot token from your Slack app, scopes: channels:manage, chat:write, users:read, bookmarks:write.

nodes.name: Build Email List
nodes.notes: Fans the filtered role emails out into one item per email so the native Slack lookup node (which runs once per item) can resolve each one individually.

nodes.name: Look up a user by email
Only Successful Lookups
nodes.notes: Drops emails that failed users.lookupByEmail (onError=continueRegularOutput upstream keeps the run alive but leaves no id field on those items) so they don't end up as undefined entries in the invite list.

nodes.name: Aggregate Slack User IDs
nodes.notes: Collapses the N resolved-user items back into a single item with userIds: [...] so Invite Users to Channel can invite everyone in one conversations.invite call.

nodes.name: Invite Users to Channel
nodes.notes: If every lookup upstream failed, userIds may end up as just the requesting user - not an error, just means nobody else got auto-invited; see Send Confirmation's warning text for how that's surfaced to the requester.

nodes.name: Recover Failed Email
nodes.notes: Look up a user by email replaces json entirely with {error: ...} on failure (onError: continueRegularOutput), losing the original email field. Recovers it via pairedItem back to Build Email List so Aggregate Failed Lookups has something to collect.

nodes.name: Aggregate Failed Lookups
nodes.notes: Collects the emails that failed users.lookupByEmail (the Filter's discarded-items output) so Send Confirmation can report which ones need a manual invite.

nodes.name: Create Drive Folder
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER: set driveId/folderId to a shared drive and template folder in your own Google Drive; requires a Google Drive credential with folder-create scope.

nodes.name: Add Bookmark - Drive
nodes.notes: Reads webViewLink from the immediate predecessor (Create Drive Folder). Reordered ahead of 'Add Bookmark - SFDC' specifically so this $json reference stays simple - don't insert another node between them without updating this reference.

nodes.name: Add Bookmark - SFDC
nodes.notes: Uses named node references (not $json), so it's unaffected by node ordering elsewhere in the workflow.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "slackChannelId": $('Create Slack Channel').item.json.id, "channelName": $('Derive Channel Variables').item.json.channelName, "oppName": $('Extract Opportunity Fields').item.json.oppName, "sfdcOpportunityId": $('Parse SFDC Opportunity ID').item.json.sfdcOpportunityId } }}

nodes.name: Register Channel with Deal Registry
nodes.notes: Represents a generic 'system of record' callback - whatever downstream tool should know a deal room now exists (a CRM custom object, a wiki page, an internal registry API, etc). Swap the URL and auth for whatever that system actually is, or delete this node if you don't have one.

nodes.name: Sync Before Confirmation
nodes.notes: Pure synchronization gate before Send Confirmation - a Merge node only fires once every connected input branch has resolved (even a branch that produced 0 items, like Aggregate Failed Lookups when nothing failed). Without this, Send Confirmation could run before the parallel failed-lookups branch finished, reading stale/incomplete data via its $('Aggregate Failed Lookups') reference. includeUnpaired=true so the confirmation still fires when the failed-lookups side has 0 items (the normal, all-succeeded case).

parameters.text: ={{ `Opportunity workspace ready: <#${$('Create Slack Channel').item.json.id}> | Drive folder: ${$('Create Drive Folder').item.json.webViewLink}` + ($('Aggregate Failed Lookups').isExecuted && $('Aggregate Failed Lookups').first().json.unresolvedEmails.length ? `\n⚠️ Could not resolve Slack accounts for: ${$('Aggregate Failed Lookups').first().json.unresolvedEmails.join(', ')} - please invite manually.` : '') }}

main.node: Look up a user by email
